queries.title: Brute Force Login Detection
queries.description: Detects repeated failed login attempts from the same source IP within a 5-minute window. Effective against automated credential stuffing and brute-force campaigns. Threshold of 10 failures is tunable.
queries.spl:
index=security (sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4625
  OR sourcetype=linux_secure "Failed password")
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count as failed_attempts by _time, src_ip, user, host
| where failed_attempts > 10
| sort -failed_attempts
| table _time, src_ip, user, host, failed_attempts

queries.title: Account Lockout Storm Analysis
queries.description: Identifies accounts locked out across the environment with source machine attribution. Distinguishes targeted lockouts from broad spray attacks by correlating source workstations.
queries.spl:
index=security sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4740
| stats count, values(dest) as locked_on, last(_time) as last_seen
    by user, src
| sort -count
| eval last_seen=strftime(last_seen, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| table user, src, count, locked_on, last_seen

queries.title: PowerShell Encoded Command Detection
queries.description: Catches PowerShell script blocks containing Base64 encoding flags or encoded payloads — a common defense evasion technique used by malware loaders, Cobalt Strike, and living-off-the-land attacks.
queries.spl:
index=endpoint sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-PowerShell/Operational"
    (EventCode=4103 OR EventCode=4104)
| search ScriptBlockText IN ("*-EncodedCommand*","*-enc *","*-e *",
    "*frombase64string*","*[Convert]::FromBase64*")
| eval encoded=if(match(lower(ScriptBlockText),"-e[nc]"),"Flag","Base64")
| stats count by host, user, encoded, ScriptBlockText
| sort -count

queries.title: Kerberoasting Attack Detection
queries.description: Identifies Kerberos TGS ticket requests using RC4-HMAC encryption (type 0x17) for service accounts — the hallmark of Kerberoasting. Attackers request these tickets offline to crack service account passwords.
queries.spl:
index=security sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4769
| where Ticket_Encryption_Type="0x17"
    AND Account_Name!="*$"
    AND Service_Name!="krbtgt"
| stats count, dc(Service_Name) as unique_svcs
    by Account_Name, Client_Address
| where count > 3
| sort -count

queries.title: DNS Data Exfiltration Detection
queries.description: Detects DNS tunneling by identifying unusually long query names and high volumes of unique queries to the same domain — classic indicators of C2 communication or data exfiltration over DNS.
queries.spl:
index=network sourcetype="stream:dns"
| eval query_length=len(query)
| where query_length > 50
| stats count, avg(query_length) as avg_len,
    dc(query) as unique_q, values(query) as sample_q
    by src_ip, dest_ip
| where count > 100 AND avg_len > 60
| sort -count

queries.title: Ransomware File Extension Monitor
queries.description: Watches file system audit events for creation of files with known ransomware extensions. Early detection allows containment before encryption spreads across the network.
queries.spl:
index=endpoint sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4663
| eval extension=lower(mvindex(split(Object_Name,"."),-1))
| search extension IN ("locky","crypto","cerber","zepto","crypt",
    "enc","locked","ransom","wnry","wncry","ryuk","darkside")
| stats count, values(Object_Name) as files,
    first(_time) as first_seen by host, user, extension
| sort -count

queries.title: Windows Event Log Clearing
queries.description: Detects when Windows Security or System event logs are cleared — a common attacker anti-forensics step performed post-compromise to remove evidence of their activity.
queries.spl:
index=security sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security"
    (EventCode=1102 OR EventCode=1100)
| eval action=case(EventCode=1102,"Audit Log Cleared",
    EventCode=1100,"Event Log Service Stopped",true(),"Unknown")
| stats count by _time, host, user, action
| sort -_time

queries.title: BITS Job Persistence Detection
queries.description: HIDDEN GEM: Background Intelligent Transfer Service (BITS) jobs survive reboots and can download/execute malware long after the initial compromise. Most teams overlook this Windows-native persistence mechanism entirely.
queries.spl:
index=endpoint
    (sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Bits-Client/Operational"
     OR (sourcetype=sysmon EventCode=1
         process_name="bitsadmin.exe"))
| search CommandLine IN ("*/transfer*","*/create*","*/addfile*")
    OR DisplayName IN ("*update*","*background*","*transfer*")
| stats count, values(DisplayName) as job_names,
    values(RemoteURL) as remote_urls by host, user, _time
| sort -_time

queries.title: Token Impersonation via OpenProcess
queries.description: HIDDEN GEM: Detects full-access process handle requests (0x1F1FFF) from Sysmon Event 10 — the signature of token theft tools like Incognito, Mimikatz token::elevate, and custom impersonation shellcode.
queries.spl:
index=endpoint sourcetype=sysmon EventCode=10
| where GrantedAccess IN ("0x1F1FFF","0x1fffff","0x1410")
    AND SourceImage!=TargetImage
| eval is_lsass=if(match(lower(TargetImage),"lsass"),"YES","no")
| stats count, values(TargetImage) as targets,
    values(SourceImage) as sources, max(is_lsass) as lsass_targeted
    by host, user
| where count > 3
| sort -count

queries.title: Lateral Movement — NTLM Pass-the-Hash
queries.description: Identifies type 3 network logons using NTLM authentication without Kerberos — the signature of pass-the-hash attacks where attackers reuse captured NTLM hashes without knowing plaintext passwords.
queries.spl:
index=security sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624
| where Logon_Type=3
    AND Authentication_Package="NTLM"
    AND Account_Name!="ANONYMOUS LOGON"
    AND NOT Account_Name LIKE "%$"
| stats count, dc(Workstation_Name) as src_hosts
    by Account_Name, IpAddress
| where src_hosts > 3
| sort -count

queries.title: Netsh Port Proxy Tunnel
queries.description: HIDDEN GEM: netsh portproxy is a Windows-native technique that forwards traffic between ports, enabling attackers to bypass firewall rules and create persistent C2 tunnels. Rarely monitored by most teams.
queries.spl:
index=endpoint sourcetype=sysmon EventCode=1
| where process_name="netsh.exe"
    AND (CommandLine LIKE "%portproxy%"
         OR CommandLine LIKE "%v4tov4%"
         OR CommandLine LIKE "%v4tov6%")
| table _time, host, user, CommandLine, parent_process_name
| sort -_time

exampleResults.CommandLine: netsh interface portproxy add v4tov4 listenport=4444 connectaddress=10.0.0.5 connectport=443
netsh interface portproxy add v4tov4 listenport=80 connectaddress=192.168.1.100 connectport=8080

queries.title: Scheduled Task Creation for Persistence
queries.description: Monitors creation and modification of Windows scheduled tasks — a top persistence technique used by ransomware, APTs, and commodity malware to survive reboots.
queries.spl:
index=security sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security"
    (EventCode=4698 OR EventCode=4702)
| eval action=if(EventCode=4698,"Created","Modified")
| rex field=Message "Task Name:s+(?P<task_name>[^
]+)"
| rex field=Message "Task Content:s+(?P<task_content>[^
]+)"
| stats count, values(task_name) as tasks, first(action) as action
    by host, user
| sort -count

queries.title: System Resource Utilization by Host
queries.description: Aggregates CPU and memory utilization across all monitored hosts, flagging those exceeding warning or critical thresholds. Essential for capacity planning and incident response.
queries.spl:
index=perfmon (sourcetype="Perfmon:CPU" OR sourcetype="Perfmon:Memory")
| eval resource=case(sourcetype="Perfmon:CPU","CPU %",
    sourcetype="Perfmon:Memory","Memory %",true(),"Other")
| stats avg(Value) as avg_pct, max(Value) as peak_pct
    by host, resource
| eval status=case(avg_pct>90,"Critical",avg_pct>75,"Warning",true(),"OK")
| sort -avg_pct

queries.title: Windows Service Failure Monitor
queries.description: Tracks Windows services that have crashed, changed state unexpectedly, or failed to start. Grouped by host and service so repeat offenders surface immediately.
queries.spl:
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:System"
    (EventCode=7034 OR EventCode=7036 OR EventCode=7043)
| eval action=case(EventCode=7034,"Service Crashed",
    EventCode=7036,"State Changed",
    EventCode=7043,"Failed to Start",true(),"Unknown")
| rex field=Message "The (?P<svc_name>.+?) service"
| stats count, values(action) as actions by host, svc_name
| sort -count

queries.title: Log Volume Anomaly Detection
queries.description: Uses statistical z-score analysis to detect sudden spikes or drops in log ingestion volume by index and sourcetype. Spikes often indicate an incident; drops indicate source failures.
queries.spl:
index=* earliest=-2d@d latest=now
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as vol by _time, index, sourcetype
| eventstats avg(vol) as avg_vol, stdev(vol) as sd_vol
    by index, sourcetype
| eval z=(vol-avg_vol)/sd_vol
| where abs(z) > 3
| sort -abs(z)
| eval direction=if(z>0,"Spike","Drop")

queries.title: Application Error Rate by Service
queries.description: Calculates error-to-total-log ratio per service in 15-minute buckets. Services breaching 5% error rate are surfaced for investigation, enabling SLO tracking and alerting.
queries.spl:
index=application
| eval is_err=if(log_level IN ("ERROR","FATAL","CRITICAL"),1,0)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats sum(is_err) as errors, count as total
    by _time, host, service_name
| eval error_rate=round(errors/total*100,2)
| where error_rate > 5
| sort -error_rate

queries.title: IIS Request Latency Analysis (p95)
queries.description: Surfaces the slowest IIS endpoints by 95th-percentile response time. The p95 metric identifies tail latency that averages hide — crucial for diagnosing user-facing performance degradation.
queries.spl:
index=iis sourcetype=iis
| where time_taken > 500
| stats avg(time_taken) as avg_ms, perc95(time_taken) as p95_ms,
    max(time_taken) as max_ms, count
    by cs_uri_stem, s_sitename
| sort -p95_ms
| table cs_uri_stem, s_sitename, avg_ms, p95_ms, max_ms, count
| head 20

queries.title: JVM Thread Pool Exhaustion Pattern
queries.description: HIDDEN GEM: Identifies JVM thread pool saturation before it causes full outages. RejectedExecutionException in logs is the earliest signal of pool exhaustion — usually missed until the app becomes completely unresponsive.
queries.spl:
index=application (sourcetype="log4j" OR sourcetype="log4net"
    OR sourcetype=java)
| search "ThreadPoolExecutor" OR "RejectedExecutionException"
    OR "thread pool exhausted" OR "Unable to process request"
| eval pool_type=case(match(_raw,"http"),"HTTP Worker",
    match(_raw,"db"),"DB Connection",
    match(_raw,"async"),"Async Task",true(),"Other")
| stats count, latest(_raw) as sample_log
    by host, service, pool_type
| sort -count

exampleResults.sample_log: java.util.concurrent.RejectedExecutionException: Task rejected from ThreadPoolExecutor@1f (pool=200, queue=500 full)
Unable to acquire JDBC Connection; nested exception: thread pool exhausted

queries.title: Transaction Volume Spike Detection
queries.description: Detects sudden spikes in transaction volumes per merchant category using a rolling 5-minute baseline comparison. Volume spikes of 3× or more indicate potential flash fraud or system abuse.
queries.spl:
index=financial sourcetype=transactions
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count as txn_count, sum(amount) as total_amount
    by _time, merchant_category
| eventstats avg(txn_count) as baseline by merchant_category
| eval spike_ratio=round(txn_count/baseline,2)
| where spike_ratio > 3
| sort -spike_ratio

queries.title: High-Value Wire Transfer Alert
queries.description: Flags wire transfers exceeding defined thresholds with risk-tier classification. Supports AML compliance by surfacing transactions that may require SAR filing or additional authorization.
queries.spl:
index=financial sourcetype=wire_transfers
| where amount > 50000
| eval risk=case(amount>1000000,"Critical",
    amount>250000,"High",true(),"Medium")
| stats count, sum(amount) as total,
    values(recipient_bank) as banks
    by sender_id, risk
| sort -total
| eval total="$"+tostring(round(total,2),"commas")

queries.title: Failed Payment Analysis by Error Code
queries.description: Breaks down payment failures by processor error code with human-readable descriptions. Helps distinguish fraud-related declines (card stolen) from operational issues (expired cards, insufficient funds).
queries.spl:
index=financial sourcetype=payment_gateway
| where status IN ("FAILED","DECLINED","ERROR")
| eval reason=case(error_code="51","Insufficient Funds",
    error_code="54","Expired Card",
    error_code="14","Invalid Card Number",
    error_code="41","Card Reported Lost",
    error_code="43","Card Reported Stolen",
    true(),"Other: "+error_code)
| stats count, dc(card_last4) as unique_cards
    by reason, merchant_id
| sort -count

queries.title: Card Velocity Fraud Detection
queries.description: HIDDEN GEM: Card-testing fraud rings make rapid low-value transactions across many merchants to validate stolen cards before large fraud. This velocity check catches multi-signal patterns that single-rule systems miss.
queries.spl:
index=financial sourcetype=transactions
| bin _time span=10m
| stats count as txn_10m,
    dc(merchant_id) as merchants,
    sum(amount) as amount_10m
    by _time, card_hash
| eval signals=if(txn_10m>5,1,0)
    +if(merchants>4,1,0)
    +if(amount_10m>5000,1,0)
| where signals >= 2
| sort -signals, -txn_10m

queries.title: Off-Hours Large Transaction Monitor
queries.description: HIDDEN GEM: Large transactions initiated between 10 PM and 7 AM carry significantly higher fraud risk. This time-aware query combines value thresholds with behavioral context that standard payment rules ignore.
queries.spl:
index=financial sourcetype=transactions
| eval hour=tonumber(strftime(_time,"%H"))
| eval off_hours=if(hour<7 OR hour>22,1,0)
| where off_hours=1 AND amount>10000
| stats count, sum(amount) as total_value,
    values(merchant_name) as merchants
    by user_id
| eval risk=if(total_value>100000,"High","Medium")
| sort -total_value

queries.title: ETL Pipeline Failure Monitor
queries.description: Aggregates failures across all ETL orchestration platforms (Airflow, Spark, Databricks) into a single view. Shows the most recently failed pipeline stage and error message for quick triage.
queries.spl:
index=data_platform sourcetype IN ("airflow","spark","databricks")
| where log_level IN ("ERROR","CRITICAL") OR status="FAILED"
| eval stage=coalesce(task_id,job_name,pipeline_name,"unknown")
| stats count, last(message) as last_error,
    latest(_time) as last_failure
    by stage, source
| sort -last_failure
| eval last_failure=strftime(last_failure,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")

exampleResults.last_error: KeyError: 'customer_id' — schema mismatch in upstream API payload
AnalysisException: Path does not exist: s3://datalake/raw/txn/2024-01-15

queries.title: Kafka Consumer Lag Monitor
queries.description: Tracks Kafka consumer group lag across topics and classifies health by threshold buckets. Growing lag indicates consumers are falling behind producers — a leading indicator of processing bottlenecks.
queries.spl:
index=kafka_metrics sourcetype=kafka_consumer
| eval health=case(consumer_lag>100000,"Critical",
    consumer_lag>10000,"High",
    consumer_lag>1000,"Medium",true(),"OK")
| stats max(consumer_lag) as max_lag,
    avg(consumer_lag) as avg_lag
    by consumer_group, topic
| sort -max_lag
| table consumer_group, topic, max_lag, avg_lag, health

queries.title: Database Slow Query Identification
queries.description: Surfaces the worst-performing database queries by average and 95th-percentile execution time. Supports MySQL slow log, PostgreSQL log, and Oracle audit formats for multi-database environments.
queries.spl:
index=database
    sourcetype IN ("mysql:slow","postgresql:log","oracle:audit")
| where query_time > 1.0
| eval db=case(sourcetype="mysql:slow","MySQL",
    sourcetype="postgresql:log","PostgreSQL",true(),"Oracle")
| stats avg(query_time) as avg_s,
    perc95(query_time) as p95_s,
    count by db, normalized_query, database_name
| sort -avg_s
| head 20

exampleResults.normalized_query: SELECT * FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON... WHERE o.status=? GROUP BY...
SELECT DATE(created_at), SUM(revenue) FROM transactions WHERE...
SELECT /*+ FULL(t) */ account_id, SUM(balance) FROM ledger t WHERE...

queries.title: API Error Spike Detection
queries.description: Detects services generating 2× or more 5xx errors compared to their rolling baseline — the standard SRE signal for a service degradation incident requiring immediate response.
queries.spl:
index=api_gateway (sourcetype=nginx OR sourcetype=kong
    OR sourcetype=envoy)
| eval class=if(status>=500,"5xx",if(status>=400,"4xx","2xx"))
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count by _time, service_name, class
| eventstats avg(count) as baseline by service_name, class
| eval spike=round(count/baseline,2)
| where class="5xx" AND spike > 2
| sort -spike

queries.title: Schema Drift Detection in Log Fields
queries.description: HIDDEN GEM: Detects when log event structure changes unexpectedly — a leading indicator of upstream application changes breaking downstream parsers. Most data teams only discover schema drift after pipelines fail.
queries.spl:
index=data_platform sourcetype=application_logs
| eval field_count=mvcount(split(_raw,","))
| bin _time span=1h
| stats avg(field_count) as avg_fields,
    stdev(field_count) as stdev_fields,
    min(field_count) as min_fields,
    max(field_count) as max_fields by _time, source
| eval drift=if(max_fields-min_fields>3,"DETECTED","ok")
| where drift="DETECTED"
| sort -_time

queries.title: HIPAA — Patient Record Access Audit
queries.description: Generates a HIPAA-compliant access audit report showing which users accessed patient records, what actions were taken, and how many unique patients were accessed — required for breach risk assessments.
queries.spl:
index=ehr
    sourcetype IN ("epic_audit","cerner_audit","allscripts_audit")
| eval access=case(action="VIEW","Read",
    action IN ("EDIT","UPDATE"),"Modify",
    action="PRINT","Print/Export",true(),action)
| stats count, dc(patient_mrn) as unique_patients,
    first(_time) as start_time
    by user, access, workstation
| sort -unique_patients

queries.title: Unauthorized PHI Access Detection
queries.description: Flags access to patient records where no treating relationship exists — the most common HIPAA violation type. Also catches staff accessing their own records, a policy violation in most health systems.
queries.spl:
index=ehr
    sourcetype IN ("epic_audit","cerner_audit")
| where treating_relationship="N"
    OR (accessed_own_record="Y" AND access_type="PRINT")
| eval risk=case(access_type="PRINT","High",
    access_type="EXPORT","Critical",true(),"Medium")
| stats count, dc(patient_mrn) as records
    by user_id, user_name, department, risk
| sort -records

queries.title: Bulk Patient Record Download Alert
queries.description: HIDDEN GEM: Most EHR breach investigations begin with individual access — but bulk exports and mass print jobs are the real data theft vectors. This query catches the high-volume exfiltration that record-by-record monitoring misses.
queries.spl:
index=ehr
    sourcetype IN ("epic_audit","cerner_audit")
| where action IN ("BULK_EXPORT","REPORT_RUN","PRINT")
    AND record_count > 100
| stats sum(record_count) as total_records,
    count as sessions,
    first(_time) as first_event
    by user_id, user_name, department
| where total_records > 500
| sort -total_records
| eval risk=if(total_records>5000,
    "CRITICAL — Possible Breach","HIGH — Review Required")

queries.title: EHR System Error Rate Monitor
queries.description: Tracks EHR system error rates by module in 30-minute buckets and flags anomalies exceeding 50% above baseline. Clinical system downtime directly impacts patient safety.
queries.spl:
index=ehr sourcetype=system_logs
    log_level IN ("ERROR","CRITICAL")
| bin _time span=30m
| stats count as errors by _time, system_name, error_category
| eventstats avg(errors) as baseline
    by system_name, error_category
| eval anomaly=round((errors-baseline)/baseline*100,1)
| where anomaly > 50
| sort -anomaly

queries.title: AWS CloudTrail — Root Account Usage
queries.description: Fires whenever the AWS root account takes any action other than console login — a CIS benchmark violation that indicates either credential compromise or improper administrative practices.
queries.spl:
index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"
    userIdentity.type=Root
| where eventName!="ConsoleLogin"
| stats count, values(eventName) as actions,
    values(sourceIPAddress) as src_ips
    by userIdentity.arn, awsRegion
| sort -count
| eval severity="CRITICAL — Root Account Active"

queries.title: Container Crash Loop Detection
queries.description: Identifies Kubernetes pods in CrashLoopBackOff with crash frequency counts and last error messages. High crash counts indicate application failures requiring immediate intervention.
queries.spl:
index=kubernetes
    (sourcetype="kube:events" OR sourcetype=kubectl:logs)
| where reason="BackOff"
    OR message LIKE "%CrashLoopBackOff%"
| stats count, latest(message) as last_msg
    by namespace, pod_name, container_name
| where count > 5
| sort -count
| eval severity=case(count>50,"Critical",
    count>10,"Warning",true(),"Info")

exampleResults.last_msg: Back-off restarting failed container: exit code 1
Back-off restarting failed container: permission denied

queries.title: AWS IAM Privilege Escalation Detection
queries.description: Detects IAM actions that could enable privilege escalation: creating users, attaching admin policies, creating access keys, or assuming powerful roles — top attacker techniques post-initial-access in AWS.
queries.spl:
index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"
| where eventName IN ("CreateUser","AttachUserPolicy",
    "PutUserPolicy","CreateAccessKey",
    "AssumeRole","AttachRolePolicy")
    AND errorCode=""
| stats count, values(eventName) as actions,
    values(requestParameters.userName) as targets
    by userIdentity.userName, sourceIPAddress, awsRegion
| sort -count

queries.title: Lambda Cold Start Latency Analysis
queries.description: HIDDEN GEM: Lambda cold starts can add 2–10 seconds to response times, invisible in average metrics. This query extracts cold start durations from CloudWatch logs to identify which functions need Provisioned Concurrency.
queries.spl:
index=aws sourcetype=aws:cloudwatchlogs
| rex "REPORT RequestId.*?Init Duration: (?P<cold_ms>[d.]+)"
| where isnotnull(cold_ms)
| eval cold_ms=tonumber(cold_ms)
| stats avg(cold_ms) as avg_cold,
    perc95(cold_ms) as p95_cold,
    count as invocations
    by function_name, memory_size
| sort -avg_cold
| eval hint=if(avg_cold>2000,
    "Use Provisioned Concurrency","Acceptable")

queries.title: Top Bandwidth Consumers (NetFlow)
queries.description: Identifies the top bandwidth consumers across the network from NetFlow data. Essential for capacity planning, detecting large file transfers, and spotting data exfiltration by volume.
queries.spl:
index=network (sourcetype=netflow OR sourcetype="palo_alto:traffic")
| eval gb_total=round((bytes_in+bytes_out)/1073741824,3)
| stats sum(gb_total) as total_gb,
    sum(packets) as total_pkts
    by src_ip, dest_ip, app
| sort -total_gb
| head 20
| eval total_gb=round(total_gb,2)+" GB"

queries.title: Network Port Scan Detection
queries.description: Detects hosts scanning more than 20 unique destination ports within a 1-minute window — the classic signature of automated port scanners like nmap. Classifies intensity as Low, Moderate, or Aggressive.
queries.spl:
index=network
    sourcetype IN ("cisco:asa","palo_alto","netflow")
| bin _time span=1m
| stats dc(dest_port) as unique_ports, count
    by _time, src_ip, dest_ip
| where unique_ports > 20
| sort -unique_ports
| eval intensity=case(unique_ports>100,"Aggressive",
    unique_ports>50,"Moderate",true(),"Low")

queries.title: ICMP Tunnel Detection
queries.description: HIDDEN GEM: ICMP tunneling tools like iodine, icmptunnel, and ptunnel embed data in ping payloads to bypass firewalls. Standard ICMP payloads are 32–48 bytes; tunneled payloads routinely exceed 200 bytes.
queries.spl:
index=network (sourcetype=zeek OR sourcetype=suricata)
| where proto="icmp"
| eval payload_size=bytes-8
| where payload_size > 64
| stats count, avg(payload_size) as avg_payload,
    sum(bytes) as total_bytes
    by src_ip, dest_ip
| where avg_payload > 200 AND count > 100
| sort -total_bytes
| eval confidence=if(avg_payload>500 AND count>500,
    "High","Medium")

queries.title: Long-Duration Connection Analysis (Beaconing)
queries.description: HIDDEN GEM: APT malware and C2 frameworks maintain persistent connections to command servers for hours or days. Connections over 12 hours with consistent data flow are strong beaconing indicators that traditional perimeter tools miss.
queries.spl:
index=network
    (sourcetype="zeek:conn" OR sourcetype=netflow)
| where duration > 3600
| eval hours=round(duration/3600,1)
| stats count, avg(hours) as avg_h,
    max(hours) as max_h,
    sum(bytes) as total_bytes
    by src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port, service
| where max_h > 12
| sort -max_h
| eval concern=if(max_h>24,
    "Beaconing Likely","Investigate")
